hook: What spending the holidays in your new home feels like. ✨
caption:
What spending the holidays in your new home feels like. ✨

There’s something about the holidays that makes you really see your home. The good and the hard both.

For a lot of people, the house that carried you through the last five years has meant everything. It’s where the memories were made, where the life got built, where so many good seasons happened. And it’s okay to love it and still feel that quiet truth: it might not be the one carrying you into what’s next.

Because here’s the thing nobody says out loud. Every good season eventually comes to an end. Not because it failed you, but because you outgrew it. And growth, real growth, almost always means a better season waiting on the other side.

So if you’ve been sitting in that in-between, grateful for what this home gave you but sensing it’s time, this is your gentle nudge. The next chapter doesn’t erase the last one. It’s built on top of it.

Imagine next year’s holidays somewhere that fits the life you have now, not the one you had five years ago.

When you’re ready to talk about what’s next, I’m here. No pressure, just a clear picture of your options.

Diana Marsh, REALTOR® and Forever Agent®
Serving move-up owners across Metro Atlanta.

hook: 🚨Metro Atlanta teachers🚨, this one is for you. 💛
caption:
🚨Metro Atlanta teachers🚨, this one is for you. 💛

Every fall, you quietly fill your own classrooms. The pencils, the folders, the tissues, the little extras that make a lesson come alive, so much of it comes out of your own pocket. You do it without being asked, because you show up for your students the way you always have. This year, let’s give some of that back to you.

I built something called Adopt a Teacher, and it is simple. Teachers add their classroom Amazon wishlist to my form. Then neighbors pick a teacher near them, grab an item off the list, and just like that, a classroom starts the year a little more ready. If you want to help a teacher, you shop their wishlist directly on Amazon. Any item, big or small, makes a difference.

This one is personal for me. My mom taught school for 40 years, and every single year she bought 20 to 30 sets of supplies for her students out of her own pocket. She is retired now, but we still have a room full of school supplies at home, because most of it retired right along with her. That is why I do this. When you touch a teacher, you touch our students, and when you touch our students, you touch our future.

Honestly, I have been wanting to do this for a while. It took a beautiful and brilliant Kansas City real estate agent doing a teacher drive like this to finally inspire me to pull the trigger and bring it home to Atlanta. @rachtherealtorkc 

Here is how to get started:
1. Comment the word “WISHLIST” below and I will send you the link.
2. Teachers, add your classroom wishlist. Helpers, pick a teacher and shop their list.
3. That is it. You just made a teacher’s year easier.

And because we stand stronger when we support our educators, I have partnered with Spavia Chamblee to give one lucky participant a relaxing spa day. Everyone who participates, teachers and helpers both, is entered to win.

We are serving Cobb, Fulton, DeKalb, Douglas, and Gwinnett counties, now through August 31.

Share this with a teacher or even just a friend! 
Comment “WISHLIST” and let’s fill some classrooms. 💛

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ 𝑫𝒊𝒂𝒏𝒂 Marsh - REALTOR®️

hook: Been in your home 3 to 5 years? Do NOT make a move until you read this. SAVE it for later and SHARE with a friend who needs to hear this from a licensed professional 😉
caption:
Been in your home 3 to 5 years? Do NOT make a move until you read this. SAVE it for later and SHARE with a friend who needs to hear this from a licensed professional 😉

1. Know your real numbers. (A Zestimate can be anywhere from 15 to 20% off in localized markets like Atlanta.)

2. Decide: sell, or keep it and rent it out.

3. Know your NET, not just your sale price.

4. Get clear on where you’re headed. Location? Neighborhood?

5. Don’t make a move before you talk to someone. Agent, strategy call, pre-approval are your first three steps.
Now the 5 most people never hear:

6. Get pre-approved BEFORE you fall in love with the next home. Knowing what you can carry changes how you shop. And being prepared to submit that offer the moment you find it changes the game.

7. Understand your timing. Can you carry two payments briefly, or do you need the sale to fund the purchase? That answer shapes everything.

8. Keeping it as a rental? Run the real numbers. Rent versus the full cost of holding it. Taxes, insurance, maintenance, vacancy. Not just the mortgage.

9. Build your team before you start, not after. The right agent and lender, in the right order, save you money and stress.

10. Don’t wait for the “perfect” moment to start the conversation. The one I’d never skip. The people who move up smoothly didn’t wait until everything felt certain. They started early and moved with a plan. Waiting costs more than starting ever will.

The ones who move up smoothly aren’t the ones with the most equity. They’re the ones who looked before they leapt.

hook: The home you bought five years ago? It might be the most important asset you own. And you’ve probably never looked at it that way.
caption:
The home you bought five years ago? It might be the most important asset you own. And you’ve probably never looked at it that way.

Here’s the truth most owners miss. After three to five years, you’ve quietly built equity, through your payments and through everything this market did. That equity is the key to your next move. The only question is how you use it.

You’ve got two doors. Sell and roll your equity into the next home. Or keep this one, rent it out, and move up anyway, so someone else helps pay down the first home while you build on the second.

Neither is automatically right. It comes down to your numbers and where you’re headed next. But one thing is certain: that home was never the finish line. It was the first step.

Curious what yours could unlock? Comment NEXT and let’s map your move together. No pressure, just a clear picture of your options.

hook: First, breathe. Nobody actually taught us this part, especially when it comes to real estate. So here’s what actually matters in the next few months.
caption:
First, breathe. Nobody actually taught us this part, especially when it comes to real estate. So here’s what actually matters in the next few months.

1: The new car or the couch upgrade can quietly shrink what you qualify for.

2: Don’t move your money around too much. Big deposits or transfers right before you buy create paperwork headaches. Let your savings sit.

3: One short conversation with a lender beats scrolling listings and guessing in your head. Just get a real number.

4: Find the payment you’ll actually sleep well with at night, not just the max they’ll give you.

5: Start watching the areas you love now, so when you’re ready, you’ll know a good deal the second you see it.

You don’t need to have it all figured out. You’re more ready than you think. You just need a plan, and someone who treats your move like it matters.

Comment “READY” and we’ll get started!

Know someone who needs to make a move in the next 3 to 6 months? Share this. They’ll thank you later.

hook: The job offer was the easy part.
caption:
The job offer was the easy part.

Now you’re staring at a map of a city you’ve barely visited, trying to figure out where your life actually fits. Which neighborhood is worth the commute. Where you’ll feel at home on a random Tuesday, not just on moving day. Whether you’re about to overpay because you don’t know the area well enough to know better.

This is the part I do every week. I help people relocate to Atlanta from all over the world, and the move always goes smoother when someone who actually knows the city is in your corner before you ever book a flight to look.

I’ll learn how you live first, your work, your routine, what matters to you, then point you to the parts of Atlanta that fit. Not the ones that look good in a listing.

You’ve got enough on your plate with this move. Let me handle the part you can’t Google.

Comment or DM me “ATLANTA”, and I’ll take it from there!

hook: Most people don’t get a second chance at a move like this.
caption:
Most people don’t get a second chance at a move like this.

You’re not buying a sweater you can return. You’re making one of the biggest financial and life decisions you’ll touch in a decade, and the cost of getting it wrong, the wrong neighborhood, the wrong price, the wrong timing, follows you for years.

That’s the part I take seriously. Before you ever tour a home or list one, I’m doing the work behind the scenes: the market data, the negotiation strategy, the things that protect your money and your time. You get to make the decision with confidence because someone handled the details right.

The people I work with tend to be busy, decisive, and used to hiring the best. They don’t want a tour guide. They want someone who treats their move like it matters as much to me as it does to them.

If that’s the kind of person you are, and you are looking to make Atlanta home, let’s talk before you make a move you can’t take back.

Comment or DM “MOVE”, and I’ll show you what working together actually looks like.

hook: LOLLLL Okay Okay! Read to the end and let me cook... I picked up a new phrase in London this weekend… “Mind the gap.” I picked up a new phrase in London this weekend… “Mind the gap.”
caption:
LOLLLL Okay Okay! Read to the end and let me cook... I picked up a new phrase in London this weekend… “Mind the gap.” I picked up a new phrase in London this weekend… “Mind the gap.”
And while they’re talking about trains, personal space, and putting too much dip on your chip 🤣 it reminded me of the two gaps buyers don’t pay attention to until it’s too late.

The biggest mistake you can make is buying with emotion or copying the shiny “hacks” you hear online.
Because in real estate, gaps get expensive.

✨ Gap 1: The Appraisal Gap
When the home appraises lower than your offer, you cover the difference. That extra money has to come out of pocket.

✨ Gap 2: The Equity Gap on Assumable Loans
Assuming a seller’s low rate sounds cute… until you realize you must pay their entire equity upfront. That gap can be huge.

When it’s a good idea:
If the long-term savings make sense and you have the cash cushion to cover the gaps without stress.

When it’s not:
If covering those gaps wipes your savings or forces you into a home that stretches you thin.

Mind the gap and move wisely.
DM me if you want to run your numbers and avoid these costly mistakes when buying a home. 🏡✨

hook: 🦃 🍁 Let's talk Thanksgiving turkey! This spiced version is sure to wow your guests with its surprisingly moist texture and punch of bold flavor.
caption:
🦃 🍁 Let's talk Thanksgiving turkey! This spiced version is sure to wow your guests with its surprisingly moist texture and punch of bold flavor. 

1. Combine these ingredients in a large stock pot to create the brine:
• 1 bottle white wine
• 2 c. water
• 2 oranges, juiced and zested
• 8 cloves
• 2 cinnamon sticks
• 2 tbsp fresh ginger, peeled and sliced
• 1/2 c. granulated sugar
• 1/2 c. salt
2. Bring the mixture to a boil, then remove it from heat and set it aside to cool. 
3. Submerge an 8–10 lb. turkey breast in the brine and refrigerate it for a minimum of 24 hours.
4. Preheat the oven to 375°F. Remove the turkey from the brine and rinse it in cool water.
5. Place the turkey in a rack-lined roasting pan, pour 1 c. turkey stock in the bottom of the pan, and roast it for 1–1.5 hours or until cooked completely.
6. Let it rest for 30 minutes before slicing and serving. Strain the pan juices and serve them with the turkey. Enjoy!

#Thanksgiving #thanksgivingrecipe #givingthanks #turkeyrecipe #rmsma

hook: 🎁 The holidays are here, and while the season is merry and bright, it can also be pricey. If you’re dreaming of buying a home, the holiday spending spree can feel like a hurdle—but it doesn’t have to
caption:
🎁 The holidays are here, and while the season is merry and bright, it can also be pricey. If you’re dreaming of buying a home, the holiday spending spree can feel like a hurdle—but it doesn’t have to derail your plans.

Here are a few quick tips to keep your financial goals on track while still enjoying the season:

1️⃣ Budget Before You Shop: Set a spending limit and stick to it. Think thoughtful, not expensive, gifts.
2️⃣ Avoid Credit Card Temptation: High balances can impact your credit score, so use cash or a debit card when possible.
3️⃣ Pause on Big Purchases: That new TV can wait until after you’ve closed on your dream home!
4️⃣ Stay Focused on Your ‘Why’: Picture yourself in your new home next holiday season—this is just a stepping stone.

The key is to spend intentionally while keeping your eyes on the prize: homeownership. Your future self will thank you!

Need more tips to navigate your finances and the homebuying process? Let’s chat—I’m here to help!

#HolidayBudgetTips #HomebuyingGoals #RealEstateJourney #ForeverAgent

hook: This week, I want to honor all the Veterans who’ve served our country!
caption:
This week, I want to honor all the Veterans who’ve served our country! 

One way I can give back is by helping Veterans take advantage of the VA Loan, which is an incredible path to homeownership.

Here’s why it’s truly a top option:

Interest rates: Currently around 6.5% for a 30-year fixed rate, which is often lower than conventional loans!

No down payment needed 💸
No PMI (Private Mortgage Insurance)
Credit flexibility: Many lenders accept credit scores as low as 580-620.

With perks like these, the VA Loan makes homeownership much more accessible. And if you’re hoping to be in your new home by the new year, it’s still possible! The process can usually be completed in 30-45 days if we start now.

If you’re a Veteran considering homeownership, I’d be honored to help you get started. Reach out anytime—I’m here to make the process as smooth and rewarding as possible.

Thank you again for all you’ve done to protect and serve. 🇺🇸

hook: As much as you’ve loved your home, isn’t it time to upgrade to something that fits your new dreams and lifestyle? ✨ Whether it’s more space, a better view, or a neighborhood that feels more like you,
caption:
As much as you’ve loved your home, isn’t it time to upgrade to something that fits your new dreams and lifestyle? ✨ Whether it’s more space, a better view, or a neighborhood that feels more like you, selling your home doesn’t have to be stressful! 🏡 Here are 9 simple steps to guide you through the home selling process, from start to finish. Let’s make the next chapter even better!

1️⃣ Find the right agent: Partner with an experienced real estate agent (like me!) who will help you navigate the entire selling process, from pricing to closing.
2️⃣ Get a market analysis: We’ll look at recent sales in your area to determine the best price for your home, so it stands out and attracts the right buyers.
3️⃣ Prep your home for sale: Time to declutter, deep clean, and make any necessary repairs or updates to showcase your home in its best light.
4️⃣ Set the right price: Pricing your home right from the start is key. I’ll help you find the sweet spot to get buyers’ attention and maximize your return.
5️⃣ Market your home: From professional photos to online listings and open houses, we’ll make sure your home gets in front of the right buyers.
6️⃣ Showings and offers: We’ll schedule showings and review offers together, ensuring we find the best deal for you.
7️⃣ Negotiate and accept an offer: I’ll handle the negotiations to make sure you get the best possible deal. Once you’re happy, we’ll accept the offer.
8️⃣ Home inspection and appraisal: Buyers will likely order an inspection and appraisal. I’ll guide you through these steps to ensure everything goes smoothly.
9️⃣ Closing day: Sign the paperwork, hand over the keys, and celebrate the sale! 🎉

Ready to move up and into your next chapter? Let’s connect! Comment "sell" or click the link in my bio for a FREE consultation.

caption:

There are so many misconceptions out there about down payment assistance, and I’m here to set the record straight! Here are three common myths that could be holding you back from your dream home:
1️⃣ Myth: Down payment assistance is only for first-time buyers.
Fact: Many programs are available for repeat buyers too! You don’t have to be a first-timer to get help.
2️⃣ Myth: You have to have a low income to qualify.
Fact: Plenty of programs are designed for middle-income earners, so don’t rule yourself out just yet!
3️⃣ Myth: It makes the buying process longer and more complicated.
Fact: With the right guidance, you can access assistance without adding extra time or stress to your home purchase.
Don’t let these myths stop you from exploring your options! You might be closer to homeownership than you think. Let’s connect and see what’s available for you. 💬✨

hook: Maintaining a positive mindset during your home search is crucial for your well-being and success. 🏡✨ In honor of World Mental Health Day, here are some tips to help you stay focused and upbeat along
caption:
Maintaining a positive mindset during your home search is crucial for your well-being and success. 🏡✨ In honor of World Mental Health Day, here are some tips to help you stay focused and upbeat along the way:

✨Reframe Your Thoughts: Celebrate your search milestones, like pre-approval or home visits.
✨Set Realistic Expectations: Prioritize "must-haves" over "nice-to-haves" and remember that finding the perfect home takes time.
✨Stay Informed: Knowledge empowers confidence, so keep learning about the market and the buying process.
✨Visualize Success: Create a vision board or journal to keep your dream home in sight.
✨Practice Patience: Trust that the right home will come at the perfect time.
✨Lean on Support: Share frustrations with friends, family, or your Realtor® for advice and emotional support.
✨Stay Flexible: Be adaptable in your search; unexpected opportunities may arise.
✨Self-Care: Prioritize your well-being through enjoyable activities, exercise, and mindfulness.
✨Stay Positive: Surround yourself with positivity and read success stories for motivation.
✨Celebrate Small Wins: Acknowledge and celebrate each achievement, no matter how small.

A positive mindset reduces stress and attracts positive outcomes. Your dream home is waiting! Wishing you a happy World Mental Health Day. 💪✨

hook: For as hard as you work, and as much as you're paying for a place to live, you should at least have a place to call your own, right? 😅 Buying a home doesn’t have to feel overwhelming! 🏡✨ Here are 9 si
caption:
For as hard as you work, and as much as you're paying for a place to live, you should at least have a place to call your own, right? 😅 Buying a home doesn’t have to feel overwhelming! 🏡✨ Here are 9 simple steps to guide you through the home buying process, from start to finish. Whether it’s your first home or your next one, these steps will help make the journey smoother and stress-free.

1️⃣ Find the right agent: Partner with a trusted real estate agent (like me!) who will guide you through the entire process and advocate for your needs.
2️⃣ Prepare finances: Get your finances in order by reviewing your budget and understanding how much you can afford.
3️⃣ Get pre-approved: Secure a pre-approval from a lender to show sellers you're a serious buyer and to know your budget.
4️⃣ Start home shopping: Begin touring homes that fit your needs and preferences. This is where the fun begins!
5️⃣ Make an offer: Once you’ve found the perfect home, work with your agent to submit a competitive offer.
6️⃣ Order inspection: A home inspection ensures that there are no hidden issues with the property before you move forward.
7️⃣ Order an appraisal: The lender will require an appraisal to confirm the home's value matches the price.
8️⃣ Schedule the move: Plan your moving day and start preparing for your exciting new chapter!
9️⃣ Closing day: Sign the paperwork, get the keys, and celebrate—your new home awaits! 🎉

Ready to start the home buying process? Let’s connect! Comment "buy" or click the link in my bio for a FREE consultation.

hook: Let’s talk about it:
caption:
Let’s talk about it: 

Closing Day is SUCH an exciting time. It never gets old and I never get tired. My sellers get a check and my buyers get their keys 🥹! 

Let’s talk about why this one was so special to me. 
We met in college. Her mom was my professor (at the time I had no idea) and the ONLY person in the department who looked like me and understood me. Over the years we became strong friends so a moment like this melted my heart.

How was I able to help her? 
- I was able to secure them a CASH OFFER guaranteed BEFORE we even hit the market. 
- We were only active on the market 24 hours before receiving our next offer. 
- Within 36 hours they had received 4 more offers AND accepted one putting us under contract.
- 3 week closing on an OVER ASKING OFFER and my clients couldn’t be happier. 

An NO, none of this was just for the sake of “selling a home” it was the first step in a plan to build a dynamic and profitable portfolio of real estate investments. 

Phase one completed. Phase two: invest invest invest.

hook: Here are 10 lesser-known neighborhoods in Atlanta that have the potential for equity growth:
caption:
Here are 10 lesser-known neighborhoods in Atlanta that have the potential for equity growth:

1. Ormewood Park: Located southeast of downtown Atlanta, Ormewood Park offers a mix of historic homes and newer construction.

2. Sylvan Hills: Situated southwest of downtown, Sylvan Hills is a hidden gem with affordable housing options and a growing sense of community.

3. Kirkwood: Nestled east of downtown Atlanta, Kirkwood is known for its historic bungalows, tree-lined streets, and sense of community.

4. Lakewood Heights: Located south of downtown Atlanta, Lakewood Heights offers affordable housing options and proximity to the Atlanta BeltLine and Lakewood Amphitheater.

5. Grove Park: Situated on Atlanta’s west side, Grove Park is undergoing revitalization efforts, including new construction and investment in community amenities.

6. Peoplestown: Adjacent to Grant Park, Peoplestown is a historic neighborhood with affordable housing options and proximity to the Atlanta BeltLine and Georgia State Stadium.

7. Chosewood Park: Located south of Grant Park, Chosewood Park offers affordable housing options and proximity to the Atlanta BeltLine and The Beacon development.

8. Westview: Situated southwest of downtown Atlanta, Westview is known for its historic homes and strong sense of community.

9. Glenrose Heights: Located southeast of downtown Atlanta, Glenrose Heights offers affordable housing options and proximity to major highways and the Atlanta Airport.

10. Adair Park: Situated southwest of downtown Atlanta, Adair Park is known for its historic homes and strong sense of community.

These neighborhoods offer potential for equity growth due to their affordability, proximity to amenities and transportation, and ongoing revitalization efforts.
